metadata.title: Replace Agile Pillars Taxonomy (L1/L2) in Database and PracticeGoal Mapping

summary.objective: Replace 5-category/19-pillar taxonomy with 4-category/14-pillar framework from docs/raw_practices/agile_pillars.md
summary.strategy: Stable slug IDs, complete reset migration, strict validation update
summary.scope: Seed script, validation enum, import mappings, test fixtures, frontend category maps, documentation

tasks.title: Define stable slug taxonomy from source of truth
Replace category and pillar seed data with new taxonomy
Implement safe DB transition strategy for FK-coupled taxonomy IDs
Update strict PracticeGoal validation to the new goals
Align practice import mappings with new category slugs
Update backend tests tied to old taxonomy IDs/names
Update additional backend tests using legacy category fixtures
Update service-level practice tests with new taxonomy fixtures
Synchronize frontend category key maps with new slugs
Update pillar/category visual mapping for new category keys
Update practice card badge mapping for new taxonomy keys
Update technical documentation to reflect new taxonomy
Update backend API docs for taxonomy values
Update frontend docs for filters and coverage categories

acceptance_criteria.statement: Given the updated seed script and an empty taxonomy state, when taxonomy seeding runs, then exactly 4 categories and 14 pillars matching docs/raw_practices/agile_pillars.md are persisted with stable slug IDs.

Given pre-existing legacy taxonomy data, when the controlled reset/reseed sequence executes, then no FK constraint error occurs and the database ends in a consistent state containing only the new taxonomy reference data.
Given a practice payload containing any legacy practice_goal value, when validation runs, then import is rejected with a validation error on practice_goal.
Given a practice payload containing only new taxonomy goals, when import runs, then all goals map to valid pillar IDs and corresponding practice_pillars links are created.
Given coverage endpoints are called after taxonomy replacement, when coverage is computed, then percentage and covered/gap logic remain unchanged and are based on the new category/pillar values.
Given backend unit/route tests are executed, when taxonomy fixtures are updated, then tests pass without changing business logic beyond taxonomy naming/ID updates.
Given frontend catalog/filters render categories and pillars, when category IDs switch to new slugs, then category filters, color badges, and grouping continue to work with no runtime errors.
Given project docs are consulted, when reading DB/API/frontend taxonomy sections, then they reflect 4 categories, 14 pillars, and slug-based IDs consistently.

risks.description: FK constraint violations if category IDs replaced without controlled cleanup
risks.mitigation: Task 3: Implement controlled reset/reseed sequence (delete practice_pillars, then update categories/pillars)

risks.description: Strict validation will intentionally reject old practice_goal values
risks.mitigation: Design feature, not bug. Future practices_reference.json refresh/reimport acknowledged out-of-scope.

risks.description: Frontend hardcoded category-key maps not synchronized with new slugs
risks.mitigation: Tasks 9-11: Update CategoryFilter, PillarFilterDropdown, PracticeCard components

risks.description: Test fixtures in 5+ files hardcoded with legacy category IDs and pillar names
risks.mitigation: Tasks 6-8: Update backend test fixtures while preserving assertion logic

integration_validation: Seed workflow count verification (4 categories, 14 pillars)
Import validation with legacy and new goal payloads
manual_verification: Practices catalog UI with category filters
No runtime errors in filter/coverage components

export_info.usage: Use Markdown for stakeholder review, JSON for programmatic workflows and versioning
